manifest.title: Nursery AEA real-data phase
manifest.description: Technical report for the Nursery/BabyWorld AEA four-arm real-data phase.

charts.title: Synchronized minus event-shuffled lift by held-out location
charts.subtitle: Mean paired-seed difference in action-balanced 2AFC accuracy, percentage points
charts.question: Is the synchronized-training lift consistent across held-out locations?
charts.rationale: Five discrete location folds are best compared as bars around an explicit zero reference.

query.sql:
SELECT
  split,
  held_out_location,
  ROUND(100.0 * AVG(lift), 4) AS mean_lift_pp,
  ROUND(100.0 * MIN(lift), 4) AS minimum_seed_lift_pp,
  ROUND(100.0 * MAX(lift), 4) AS maximum_seed_lift_pp,
  COUNT(*) AS paired_seed_count,
  MAX(test_windows) AS test_windows,
  MAX(supported_action_count) AS supported_action_count,
  MIN(audit_valid) AS audit_valid
FROM paired_location_lifts
GROUP BY split, held_out_location
ORDER BY held_out_location
query.description: Aggregates paired synchronized-minus-shuffled seed differences within held-out location folds.
filters: paired synchronized and shuffled seed cells only

blocks.body:
## Technical summary

**Status:** `infrastructure_smoke_test_not_a_real_data_finding`. This is an infrastructure smoke test on real AEA windows, not a real-data effect finding.

**Data represented:** 247 accepted windows from 28 recordings across 5 locations.

**Primary synchronized − event-shuffled estimate:** +0.08 pp (95% CI -0.06, +0.24). The claim gate did not pass.

## The location-fold pattern is diagnostic, not developmental evidence

Each bar is the mean paired synchronized-minus-shuffled difference within one held-out location. Positive values favor synchronized IMU during training; the zero line marks no lift. The chart is a smoke diagnostic whenever the report status says so, regardless of sign.

## Scope, population, and metric definitions

AEA contains adult, partly scripted activities and is used only as a sensor-format analogue. It is not developmental evidence and is not BabyView-matched. The primary endpoint is action-balanced 2AFC macro accuracy on RGB plus transcript with IMU absent from the evaluation batch. The primary estimand subtracts a split-local whole-event-shuffled IMU arm from the correctly synchronized arm.

## Evaluation families stay separate

Held-out location is primary. Wearer-session-proxy and action-object-composition results are secondary and retain separate uncertainty intervals so they cannot silently replace the locked endpoint.

| Family | Splits | Mean lift, pp | 95% CI, pp | Paired seeds |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| held_out_composition | 3 | +1.08 | [-1.33, +4.58] | 2 |
| held_out_location | 5 | +0.08 | [-0.06, +0.24] | 2 |
| held_out_wearer_session | 5 | +0.38 | [-0.10, +1.67] | 2 |

## Four arms isolate synchronization during training

Null, synchronized, split-local event-shuffled, and time-shifted arms share architecture, initialization, batch order, optimizer schedule, and update count within paired seeds. Concurrent recordings of the same performed event share a donor group. The primary test path constructs no motor tensor and never calls the motor encoder. Confidence intervals resample held-out split units and paired seeds hierarchically.

## Claim controls remain closed unless every lock passes

The audit below checks the leakage, pairing, training-schedule, completion, and motor-withholding conditions that determine whether an estimate is eligible for interpretation.

| Eligibility check | Passed |
|---|---:|
| Leakage and pairing gates | yes |
| Locked training configuration | no |
| Complete locked folds and seeds | no |
| IMU omitted from primary test | yes |
| Claim gate | no |

## Sensor coverage is audited before modeling

The preprocessing audit checks acquisition completeness, finite six-axis arrays, fixed resampled shape, RGB timestamp error, allowlisted model inputs, and absence of unnecessary MPS features.

| Planned | Available VRS | Processed | Accepted windows | Minimum IMU coverage | Max RGB error | Sensor/file errors |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 40 | 40 | 28 | 247 | 100.00% | 24.54 ms | 0 |

## Limitations prevent a developmental claim

Action targets are ASR lexical anchors rather than human action annotations; object targets are nearby lexical items and may not name the manipulated object. The wearer split uses a release-visible location+script+recording proxy, not persistent identity. Location 5 covers only scripts 4 and 5. AEA cannot establish a benefit in infants or in natural BabyView data.

## Recommended next steps

1. Treat smoke numbers only as pipeline validation.
2. Run the complete locked five-fold, seven-seed, eight-epoch primary schedule before using `real_aea_effect_estimate`.
3. Inspect positive controls and per-action support before interpreting any aggregate.
4. Use the AEA result to refine the apples-to-apples BabyView protocol, not as developmental evidence.

## Further questions

Would human-verified action/object annotations change the estimate? Does lift vary with head-motion intensity, speech timing, or action class? Which sensor-format differences must be calibrated before an eventual BabyView comparison?

query.sql:
SELECT
  split,
  held_out_location,
  ROUND(100.0 * AVG(lift), 4) AS mean_lift_pp,
  ROUND(100.0 * MIN(lift), 4) AS minimum_seed_lift_pp,
  ROUND(100.0 * MAX(lift), 4) AS maximum_seed_lift_pp,
  COUNT(*) AS paired_seed_count,
  MAX(test_windows) AS test_windows,
  MAX(supported_action_count) AS supported_action_count,
  MIN(audit_valid) AS audit_valid
FROM paired_location_lifts
GROUP BY split, held_out_location
ORDER BY held_out_location
